name: Hoces del Rio Piedra y Barranco de Montecillo desde Aldehuela de Liestos — trail running por el GR 24
name_original: Hoces del río Piedra y Barranco de Montecillo desde Aldehuela de Liestos — trail running por el GR 24
region_intro: Jogging around Campo de Daroca offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by expansive natural features and historical sites. The region features varied ecosystems, from wetlands like Gallocanta Lagoon to the rugged Hoces del Río Piedra gorges and serene Mediterranean forests. Runners can explore trails that wind through medieval towns, past ancient walls, and alongside unique natural phenomena like Aguallueve de Anento. This blend of natural beauty and historical charm provides a tranquil backdrop for physical activity. Best jogging routes around Campo de Daroca The most popular jogging route is Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the picturesque setting around Anento, known for its unique water formations. Another top favourite among local runners is Hoces del Rio Piedra y Barranco de Montecillo desde Aldehuela de Liestos — trail running por el GR 24, a difficult 12.6 miles (20.2 km) path. This trail offers varied terrain through the scenic gorges of the Piedra River, following parts of the GR 24. Local runners also love the Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Daroca, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through the historic town of Daroca and its surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Jogging around Campo de Daroca is defined by medieval walls, significant wetlands, and river gorges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Campo de Daroca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Campo de Daroca?
A: Campo de Daroca offers a diverse landscape for runners. You'll find trails winding through historical towns with medieval walls, alongside significant wetlands like the Gallocanta Lagoon, and through rugged river gorges such as the Hoces del Río Piedra. The region also features serene Mediterranean forests and unique natural phenomena like the Aguallueve de Anento, providing varied scenery and surfaces for your run.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier jog?
A: While many routes in Campo de Daroca are considered difficult, there are several moderate options perfect for a less strenuous run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983227069/aguallueve-de-anento-y-arguilay-de-baguena-trail-running-circular-desde-el-castillo-de-anento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.3 km trail that explores the picturesque setting around Anento. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.8 km route through the historic town.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running routes in the Campo de Daroca region?
A: Absolutely. Campo de Daroca is well-suited for challenging trail running, with 10 routes rated as difficult. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983220057/hoces-del-rio-piedra-y-barranco-de-montecillo-desde-aldehuela-de-liestos-trail-running-por-el-gr-24" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Rio Piedra y Barranco de Montecillo desde Aldehuela de Liestos — trail running por el GR 24&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 20.2 km path that offers varied terrain through the scenic gorges of the Piedra River.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical landmarks or interesting sights?
A: Yes, many routes incorporate the rich history of the region. You can run along the impressive medieval walls of Daroca, dating back to the 13th and 14th centuries. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to explore the historic town. Additionally, the region features charming villages like Anento with well-preserved heritage.

Q: Where can I experience unique natural phenomena while jogging?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983227069/aguallueve-de-anento-y-arguilay-de-baguena-trail-running-circular-desde-el-castillo-de-anento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento&lt;/a&gt; route takes you near the unique natural phenomenon of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, where water emerges from rock formations. This area, close to one of Spain's most beautiful villages, offers a picturesque backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there running trails where I can observe wildlife, especially birds?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural monument and a vital wetland, renowned as a major stopover for migratory birds, particularly cranes. The flat terrain around the lagoon provides accessible routes for jogging, allowing for excellent birdwatching opportunities. You can also find rupicolous birds, including a large vulture colony, in the Hoces del Río Piedra area.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Campo de Daroca?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Campo de Daroca are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983227069/aguallueve-de-anento-y-arguilay-de-baguena-trail-running-circular-desde-el-castillo-de-anento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt;, which allow you to explore without retracing your steps.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Campo de Daroca?
A: The tranquil atmosphere and expansive landscapes of Campo de Daroca make it suitable for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be warm, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable, while winters are typically mild enough for running, especially on sunny days.

Q: Are the running routes in Campo de Daroca generally dog-friendly?
A: Campo de Daroca's natural and open landscapes are generally welcoming for dogs. Most trails are unpaved paths through forests, fields, and along riverbeds, which are suitable for running with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, especially near wildlife or livestock, and check local regulations for specific areas or protected zones.

Q: What are some scenic spots for running in the region?
A: For breathtaking views, consider routes around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, especially at sunrise or sunset, where you can also enjoy birdwatching. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983220057/hoces-del-rio-piedra-y-barranco-de-montecillo-desde-aldehuela-de-liestos-trail-running-por-el-gr-24" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Río Piedra&lt;/a&gt; offers rugged beauty with rock formations and diverse forests. Running along the medieval walls of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca&lt;/a&gt; provides a unique blend of historical and natural scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that take me through charming villages?
A: Yes, several running routes in Campo de Daroca offer the chance to explore charming villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983227069/aguallueve-de-anento-y-arguilay-de-baguena-trail-running-circular-desde-el-castillo-de-anento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento&lt;/a&gt; route starts near Anento, recognized as one of Spain's most beautiful villages, allowing you to experience its well-preserved historical heritage.

Q: How many runners typically explore the trails in Campo de Daroca using komoot?
A: The running trails in Campo de Daroca are popular among the komoot community. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain of the region, indicating a vibrant and active running scene in the area.
